title: Hello & Who I Am
focus: Greet and respond, introduce myself, give personal information, spell my name.
function: Introduce myself and give personal information
foundationalLiteracy: The alphabet as names of letters, not yet sounds. Recognize and write my own name accurately. Letter formation for the letters in my name and country.
culturalNote: Name order varies by culture — in many countries the family name comes first. Ask how a student wants to be called and say it correctly; a mispronounced name on day one is the fastest way to lose a newcomer. Some students have a name they use at home and one assigned at a border or camp. Let them choose.

1.canDo: Point to the right person or photo when greeted or named aloud.
1.scaffold: Photo cards of classmates on the desk. Teacher says one name; student points. Gesture accepted as a full answer.
1.check: Points to the correct photo in 4 of 5 trials.
1.l1Bridge: Preview hello, name, and from in the home language; pointing to the photo is a full answer.

2.canDo: Identify a person's name and country from a short spoken introduction.
2.scaffold: Two-column picture grid (names / flags). Audio replays on demand at slowed rate.
2.check: Marks the correct name and country from a 3-sentence spoken introduction.
2.l1Bridge: Give country and language names in the home language first, then English, so only the frame is new.

3.canDo: Follow a multi-part spoken introduction and match details to the right people.
3.scaffold: Note-catcher grid with three columns. Audio plays twice at normal rate.
3.check: Correctly matches 3 speakers to name, country, and language from connected speech.
3.l1Bridge: Note-catcher may be annotated in the home language; the matching task stays in English.

1.canDo: Say a one-word greeting and my name.
1.scaffold: Echo an audio model. Word bank with photo support. A wave counts as the greeting.
1.check: Produces a greeting plus own name intelligibly to a partner.
1.l1Bridge: Student may greet in the home language first, then echo the English model. Both count.

2.canDo: Give a 2–3 sentence self-introduction using a model.
2.scaffold: Sentence frames with three slots (name / country / language) and a world map.
2.check: Produces 3 framed sentences with correct personal information.
2.l1Bridge: Rehearse the three-slot frame silently in the home language before producing it in English.

3.canDo: Introduce myself and ask a partner one question back.
3.scaffold: Open frame plus one modeled question. No word bank required.
3.check: Introduces self in connected speech and initiates one question to a partner.
3.l1Bridge: Plan the reciprocal question in the home language; deliver it in English.

1.canDo: Recognize my own name and the words hello, name, from with picture support.
1.scaffold: Word–picture matching cards. Own name shown alongside three distractors.
1.check: Selects own written name from 4 options; matches 3 core words to pictures.
1.l1Bridge: If the home language uses a different script, show the student's own name in both.

2.canDo: Read a labeled ID card and find name, country, and language.
2.scaffold: Highlighter task on a large-print ID card with icon labels on each field.
2.check: Locates and highlights all three fields on an unfamiliar ID card.
2.l1Bridge: ID-card field labels (name, country, language) glossed in the home language.

3.canDo: Read a short peer profile and match it to the correct photo.
3.scaffold: Three 2-sentence profiles, three photos. Key vocabulary glossed in the margin.
3.check: Matches 3 of 3 profiles to photos using details, not guessing.
3.l1Bridge: Home-language glossary for the three profiles; the matching is done in English.

1.canDo: Trace and copy my name and country onto a name-tent.
1.scaffold: Dotted-line tracing guide, then a copy line. Model card stays in view.
1.check: Produces a legible name-tent with own name spelled correctly.
1.l1Bridge: Where the home script differs, practice the Latin-alphabet form alongside the original.

2.canDo: Complete a personal-information form (name, country, language).
2.scaffold: Labeled form with icons and a word bank of country and language names.
2.check: Completes all three fields legibly and in the correct slots.
2.l1Bridge: Form labels glossed in the home language; the student writes in English.

3.scaffold: Starter capitals provided; punctuation reminder strip at the top of the page.
3.check: Writes 3 sentences with capital letters on names and end punctuation.
3.l1Bridge: Draft one sentence in the home language, then write all three in English.

1.scaffold: Word bank + photo cards. Traces a frame, fills one blank, echoes an audio model. Gesture routine for hello.

2.say: Hello. My name is [name]. I am from [country]. I speak [language].
2.scaffold: Sentence frames + a world map. Fills three slots, produces the string aloud from the model.

3.say: Hi, my name is [name]. I'm from [country], and I speak [language] and a little English. [question]
3.scaffold: Open frame + one modeled question. Extends independently and initiates an exchange with a partner.
